cocoon plugin pin — emit an enable entry for [plugins].enable (and optionally [plugins.methods])

By default a `"<id>=<ref>"` array element is printed to stdout for
you to add to the [plugins].enable array in workspace.toml. A bare version
(1.23.4) is written as an exact pin (<id>=1.23.4); pass "latest" to track
the newest release. With --write the entry is upserted in place (added, or
the existing <id> element is replaced); the rest of the file is preserved
verbatim.

Versions are frozen to concrete releases — and per-arch SHA256 checksums
recorded — by `cocoon lock` into the lock file; checksums no longer
live in workspace.toml.

Pass --method <name> for plugins that declare two or more entries under
[install.methods] in their plugin.toml — the pin then writes (or prints)
both: the `"<id>=<ref>"` enable entry and a
`<id> = "<method>"` line for [plugins.methods].

Usage:
  cocoon plugin pin <id> <ref> [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help            help for pin
      --method string   install method name; only meaningful for plugins that declare [install.methods]. When set, the command pins both [plugins.methods] and the [plugins].enable entry in a single workspace.toml read-write. When omitted, only the enable entry is updated (the existing [plugins.methods] entry — or the plugin's default_method — stays in effect).
      --write           upsert the enable entry in workspace.toml (auto-discovered from cwd)
